title: PCIe 7 Switch IP with Time Division Multiplexing: Powering the Next Generation of AI Connectivity - Semiwiki
gist: Rambus, which grew its PCIe interface IP business 29% year-over-year per SemiWiki's Interface IP Survey, launched PCIe 7 Switch IP with integrated Time Division Multiplexing that dynamically allocates bandwidth (rather than fixed allocation) for AI networking SoCs used in SmartNICs and SuperNICs, as PCIe 7.0 doubles per-lane bandwidth to 128 GT/s.

themes.theme: Rambus interface IP growth and new PCIe 7 switch product

insights.claim: According to SemiWiki's Interface IP Survey report, Rambus achieved 29% year-over-year growth in its PCIe interface IP business, which the company is building on with a new PCIe 7 Switch IP featuring integrated Time Division Multiplexing (TDM) for next-generation AI networking SoCs.
insights.speaker: Kalar Rajendiran
insights.so_what: A named 29% YoY growth figure in a specific IP category (PCIe) is a concrete data point for tracking Rambus's interface-IP market share gains amid the broader AI-driven connectivity IP expansion.
insights.quote: Rambus achieved 29% year-over-year growth in its PCIe interface IP business, highlighting its growing presence in the high-speed connectivity IP market.

insights.claim: PCIe 7.0 doubles the data rate from 64 GT/s to 128 GT/s per lane, but the article argues that as bandwidth increases, efficiently sharing it among many simultaneously active devices and maintaining predictable latency become just as important as raw throughput, which is what Rambus's TDM-enabled switch is designed to address.
insights.speaker: Kalar Rajendiran
insights.so_what: Quantifies the generational bandwidth jump (64->128 GT/s) that is driving AI cluster interconnect upgrades, and frames bandwidth-sharing efficiency (not just raw speed) as the next competitive axis for switch IP vendors.
insights.quote: PCIe 7.0 doubles the data rate from 64 GT/s to 128 GT/s per lane, providing the bandwidth needed for increasingly data-intensive AI workloads.

insights.claim: Time Division Multiplexing is not part of the PCIe 7.0 specification itself but an architectural enhancement Rambus implemented within its switch, dynamically allocating transmission opportunities based on application demand rather than assigning fixed bandwidth to connected devices.
insights.speaker: Kalar Rajendiran
insights.so_what: Clarifies that TDM is a proprietary Rambus differentiator layered on top of the open PCIe 7.0 standard, not a standard feature — relevant for assessing whether this is a defensible IP moat versus something competitors can replicate within spec.
insights.quote: Importantly, TDM is not part of the PCIe 7.0 specification; it is an architectural enhancement implemented within the switch itself.
insights.locator: Time Division Multiplexing Improves Bandwidth Efficiency

themes.theme: SmartNICs/SuperNICs as the new AI connectivity chokepoint

insights.claim: PCIe switch IP is increasingly embedded within advanced networking SoCs used in SmartNICs and the emerging class of SuperNICs, which are purpose-built for AI, supporting high-performance communication among thousands of GPUs and accelerators operating in tightly synchronized training environments.
insights.speaker: Kalar Rajendiran
insights.so_what: Identifies SuperNICs as an emerging, AI-specific networking device category distinct from general-purpose SmartNICs — a category worth tracking for IP licensing and silicon design-win opportunities as AI clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs.
insights.quote: SuperNICs are purpose-built for AI, supporting high-performance communication among thousands of GPUs and accelerators operating in tightly synchronized training environments.

insights.claim: Rambus's PCIe 7 Switch IP provides flexible lane partitioning where a single x16 interface can be configured at system initialization as one x16 connection, two x8 connections, or four x4 connections, letting designers adapt the same hardware platform to different combinations of GPUs, SSDs, accelerators, and networking devices.
insights.speaker: Kalar Rajendiran
insights.so_what: Configurable lane partitioning reduces the need for multiple SoC variants per system configuration, a concrete cost/flexibility benefit for AI networking SoC designers evaluating Rambus IP versus competitors.
insights.quote: A x16 interface, for example, can be configured during system initialization as one x16 connection, two x8 connections, or four x4 connections, allowing system designers to adapt the same hardware platform for different combinations of GPUs, SSDs, accelerators, and networking devices.
insights.locator: Time Division Multiplexing Improves Bandwidth Efficiency
